key_ideas: Information set decoding is central to code-based cryptanalysis and parameter selection.
Applicability validators must distinguish code parameters and decoding assumptions from lattice targets.
paper_cards.relevance: Future code-based adapters should model ISD-style cost estimates and memory/time tradeoffs without borrowing lattice assumptions.
paper_cards.risk: Estimator and parameter mappings require code-based cryptanalysis review before any score is produced.
paper_cards.title: Attacking and Defending the McEliece Cryptosystem

authors: National Institute of Standards and Technology
key_ideas: SLH-DSA is a stateless hash-based signature standard based on SPHINCS+.
Toy hash-bound or chain-verification fixtures must not be generalized to SLH-DSA security claims.
Applicability validators need algorithm revision, parameter-set, and vector provenance gates before any public benchmark result.
paper_cards.relevance: Future hash-based adapters should separate toy WOTS-chain plumbing from reviewed SLH-DSA parameter, vector, and conformance boundaries.
paper_cards.risk: Requires hash-based signature expert review before any adapter, vector bundle, or conformance claim is implemented.
paper_cards.title: NIST FIPS 205 Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Standard

authors: National Institute of Standards and Technology
key_ideas: KAT and ACVP-style vector checks are correctness evidence, not cryptanalytic evidence.
Timing and benchmark summaries are environment-specific unless device, toolchain, runner, and provenance are pinned.
The public verifier must stay JSON-only until an isolated runner and redaction protocol exist.
paper_cards.relevance: Future implementation-security hooks should propose KAT, timing, and benchmark-harness work only through pinned public artifacts and explicit no-execution review gates.
paper_cards.risk: Requires implementation-security expert review before any live artifact, device, ACVP, timing, or benchmark adapter is implemented.
paper_cards.title: Public PQC Implementation Harness Boundaries

key_ideas: Historical isogeny attacks belong in a separate plugin with explicit non-current-standard boundaries.
Toy path reconstruction fixtures can test routing and reporting without solving real isogeny problems.
Reports must prevent historical break narratives from being projected onto unrelated current standards.
paper_cards.relevance: Future historical-isogeny adapters should represent SIDH/SIKE lessons as bounded historical reconstruction fixtures, not as current PQC security claims.
paper_cards.risk: Requires isogeny expert review before any historical reconstruction adapter is expanded beyond bounded public toy fixtures.
paper_cards.title: An efficient key recovery attack on SIDH

key_ideas: Multivariate schemes need applicability validators for variables, equations, fields, and algebraic attack models.
Historical Rainbow-style lessons should be represented as benchmarks, not generalized to unrelated schemes.
paper_cards.relevance: Future multivariate adapters should represent MinRank and algebraic-solver hypotheses as review-gated operators.
paper_cards.risk: Algebraic cost models are family-specific and require review; schema-only placeholders must not emit fake estimates.
paper_cards.title: Breaking Rainbow Takes a Weekend on a Laptop

key_ideas: Module-lattice reduction modeling may differ from plain lattice heuristics.
Any estimator impact should remain review-gated.
paper_cards.relevance: Potential operator/hypothesis for module-lattice reduction cost models.
paper_cards.title: Predicting Module-Lattice Reduction

key_ideas: Do not use evaluator score alone as proof.
paper_cards.relevance: Future work should compare evolved strategy parameters against public LWE attack baselines.
paper_cards.title: SALSA: Attacking Lattice Cryptography with Transformers

key_ideas: Use public benchmark instances rather than private or live targets.
Separate benchmark reproduction from estimator-only scoring.
paper_cards.relevance: Public LWE benchmark data can inform future reproduction ladders.
paper_cards.risk: Dataset mapping requires careful review before benchmark claims.
paper_cards.title: TAPAS / LWE Benchmarking
